revision.content:
== Saturday, 26 July 2025 from 9am to 3pm ==
Join us at Bloominglabs to learn the fundamentals of 3D printing!

This workshop is open to members and non-members alike, ages 14 and up. This is an all-day workshop.

We'll be starting with ready-made 3d models. '''''This is not a CAD or modelling class.'''''

We'll begin with an overview of the process, learn to slice models into gcode, then print.

== Sign up ==
Sign up [https://www.paypal.com/ncp/payment/7U3AQFU4FLMZJ here via paypal]. The cost is $40 per seat (guardians of minors don't need to register unless they're attending themselves). Limit 12 persons.

== Schedule ==
We'll begin with a discussion of fundamentals to get started with a first print.

Then, once we have our first print going, we'll circle back to other topics.

12-12:40 Lunch break (you can run out or eat in, we'll leave prints running)

After lunch will be less structured. We'll do Q&A, continue to set up prints, and cover more advanced concepts.

== What to bring ==
Bring a laptop if you prefer to work on your own machine. We'll provide some laptops to share, so this isn't necessary. 

Bring some models you want to print. Try to limit the size to about one clenched fist. Larger prints can take longer than we have available in the workshop. (We can also scale a model down to a manageable size as well). This isn't necessary either as we'll have some examples to choose from.

You can find plenty of free-to-use content at [https://www.printables.com printables] or [https://www.thingiverse.com thingiverse].

There's fridge space available if you bring a lunch.

== What you can expect to learn ==

* Common plastics and their strengths (PLA, PETG, ABS/ASA)
* The different types of machine geometries (Cartesian, CoreXY, Delta)
* How to slice models into gcode
* How to optimize slicing for speed, strength, surface finish, or fitment
* How to pick nozzle size and the effects on your print time and surface finish
* Whether you want to build or buy your next machine
* What features are present in cheap vs expensive printers and the real experience of each
* How to assess and improve dimensional accuracy of your printer
* How to evaluate and diagnose print failures or defects
* How to use common machine firmware (Marlin, Klipper, and Vendor versions of each)

== About the Instructors ==
Adam Nuwer and Isaac Clausman have a combined 20 years between them of printing mayhem. They have made spaghetti on a dozen different machines, fried most types of control boards and learned the true meaning of thermal runaway.

Please direct any questions to Adam at adam@pewpewspaceship.com
